c1.speaker: julian
c1.line: Mr. Gibbons — of the Ledger, of course. Tonight belongs to the whole district, and I intend to say so, at reasonable length, with two jokes my sister has already vetoed.

press.line: Twenty-four this week, and the chair with it. Ready?

empathize.line: Big week for you. Your father would have liked seeing it.

observe.line: You spin that ring when you talk about tonight.

c3p.speaker: julian
c3p.line: Born ready — that's the phrase, isn't it? Though I've always wondered about it. Nobody's born ready. Some of us are barely ready by the ceremony.

c3e.speaker: julian
c3e.line: ...Yes. He would have stood just there, I think, and let Warren have the stage anyway. Father was generous like that. It made following him look easy, which was his one dishonesty.

c3o.speaker: julian
c3o.line: Do I? Habit. It was Father's. It's a little loose on me — deer fingers run slim in our line. I should have it resized. I keep not having it resized.

c4.speaker: julian
c4.line: Tonight, then. History gets opened, speeches get survived, and tomorrow the district wakes up exactly the same, only prouder. That's the plan.
